description:
Jacob, a comic figure drawn by Sven Hartmann, is a cat. He has 4 animal friends, Pump, Flap, Pingi and Patsch - all are seen here.

The game is played on a 9x8 board. 20 tiles show 4 x Jacob and his 4 friends. The tiles are randomly placed on the board face down. Each player puts the playing piece of his neighbor on an empty space of the board and the game can begin.

Object of the game is to collect 4 tiles with each friend of Jacob. The player who achieves this first, is the winner of the game.

Movements of the playing pieces are handled in a very unusual way: each row of the board shows a number, which indicates the distance that a pieces must move (in any direction). If a piece lands on a tile, the player looks at it secretly and keeps it, if he does not have it already. If he finds a piece already in his collection, he puts it back on a empty space of his choice. This can not only confuse the player's memory, it can also be made in a more strategical way, because the movements of the playing pieces are defined by the already mentioned number of the rows.

A tennis game where the outcome of each shot is determined by rolling a die. Each ball is a series of rolls of the five different dice. There is one die for serves, one for second serve, one for baseline play, one for short balls and one for high balls.

There are no choices to be made. The dice are rolled back and forth between the two players until someone wins the ball. With this tool for determine balls, matches can be played according to normal tennis rules. There are no rules for which of the players of one side is rolling dice when, when playing doubles.

From package (rough translation from Swedish): "With Grand Slam Tennisspel you can play tennis wherever you are. You can play single or double. Always exciting."

The players are relatives of aunt Luzie, who is soon going to die. Aim of the game is to inherit some of aunt Luzie's wealth.
To get to her money the players have a set of legacy-hunter-cards, from which 5 cards are drawn in the beginning. They are gifts for aunt Luzie. The inheritance cards are shuffled and placed in the middle of the board.
Every turn the players give aunt Luzie gifts like journeys or parties in form of the legacy-hunter-cards, which have a certain value from 1-6. Every player is allowed to give one card. In the 2nd phase another card can be placed to increase chances or to pick another category. After all players have placed cards aunt Luzie is moved around (dice) and according where she lands the player with the highest card(s) in that category inherits something.
The player with the most valuable inheritance cards in the end wins.

Encounter at Hanover is a promotional game for the town of Hanover, Pennsylvania, sponsored by the Hanover General Hospital Auxiliary.

Hanover is mostly famous for being the site of an American Civil War battle in the Gettysburg campaign.  While the name of the game (the same as a book on the battle) and the box cover (which shows ACW soldiers) suggest a wargame of some kind, the game is in fact similar to Monopoly.

As in Monopoly players move around the board buying properties and improving them.  Here the properties are local Hanover business and the improvements are franchises instead of houses .  The rules are similar to Monopoly's with some exceptions, the main one being that ownership of a group is not required for development, but owning three properties in a row triples rent on them.

From the box cover: "An exciting game of skill that challenges your power to read minds. Match wits with your opponent and be the first to decode the 'secret word' or color. Can be played in any language between two players. Age group 7 years and above."

This game plays like Mastermind, but in addition to colors, words can also be used.

Four real, plush panda bears are included. The bears must gather apples, oranges, pears, and plums as they climb through the trees. But they must also be careful not to fall out. Is that branch strong enough to hold a panda? How high can you go? It's easy to cuddle up to this game. Hold on tight!

This is a game for ages 12 and up. the box contains a board, 12 small pawns, 12 large pawns, 3 turntables and 24 cards.

The board shows 6 colored rows, with each 18 fields. The rows are placed parallel to one another on the board. Two start and two destination fields are located on the ends of each row.

The turntables have each a different amount of fields. The first one only shows values from 1 to 3. The second one shows the values 3, 5, 7, 9 and several times 0. The third one can be used to try to double the number of fields your pawns can move.

There are 3 categories of cards : joker, super joker and stop cards.

For starters, each player gets 4 pawns (2 small and 2 large)of one color and 4 cards (2 joker, 1 super joker and 1 stop card). The pawns are placed on their start fields.

On your turn you can turn one of the two turntables with numbers. You can move one of your pawns accordingly. If you want to, you can turn the third turntable and try to double the distance your pawn can move.

A field can never contain more than one pawn. You can leap over your pawns.

If there are pawns on the fields parallel to the one where a large pawn is landing, the pawns on the parallel fields have to restart. Little pawns can never cause a restart to other pawns.

You can influence the course of the game by using the cards.  Playing a joker befor you turn one of the turntables will double your result. The superjoker can be played after you have turned one of the turntables to double the result. The stop card can be used to stop one of the other players during his turn, but only when that player didn't play a joker or superjoker.

The game ends as soon as one of players has placed his 4 pawns on his destination fields.

This is a game for ages 10 and up. The theme is based on the TV show with the same name.

The aim of the game is to earn as much money as possible. You can achieve that by buying and selling cattle, oil sources or farmland. Other sources of income are the rents for ranches. These rents are higher for more expensive ranches and are getting even higher if the owner owns several ranches.

If you succeed in buying and selling often, you will gain money more easily.

The game is played on a board comparable with a Monopoly board. The game mechanics are the same as those used for playing Monopoly.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The game "Armageddon" was included in Spielbox 3/83 as part of the "Spiel zum Herausnehmen/Game to Remove" series. The introduction refers to Armageddon: Das strategische Fantasyspiel, because the Spielbox game plays in the same fantasy world, "Magira".

Armageddon has rules for a simplified game as well as for an advanced game. Both versions are played on a 17×20 hex board showing Magira with 1 fortification in the middle and 2 on the sides.

The easier version is played with 12 axe men, 12 pikemen and 12 swordsmen only. Object of the game is to capture the fortress in the middle of the board. All types of warriors have the same movement factor. Combats are decided by the throw of 1 die, but several bonus point are added to the throw. One of them is a kind of Rock-Scissors-Paper effect, because an axeman fighting a pikeman, a pikeman against a swordsman and a swordsman against an axeman gets 1 bonus point.

The advanced version adds several new units (archers, horsemen, veterans, horses), enables ranged attacks, combat support possibilities and faster movements. The victory condition stays the same.

There is also a variation, where a siege of a fortress is simulated.

The game "Quadriga" was included in Spielbox 4/81 and is the first game of the "Spiel zum Herausnehmen/Game to Remove" series.

It depicts the chariot races in ancient Rome. Up to 8 teams race around the track twice to determine victory.

Several obstacles and narrow curves make it difficult to drive the chariots. Therefore, accidents are likely to occur and since completely destroyed chariots stay on the track where eliminated, the difficulties even rise further.

Included to the game is an additional scenario. It is based on the novel "Sagare" from Tanith Lee and it includes many aspects from that novel: earth, air, water and fire obstacles as well as furious archers.

The game "Napoleon" was included in Spielbox 6/86 as part of the "Spiel zum Herausnehmen/Game to Remove" series.

Thought as an introduction to Cosim games, this game has simplified wargame rules. Anyway, the Allied and French forces enter play according to a detailed time plan.

The hex board is somehow exchangeable for Napoleonic battles, because no village names are marked on it and the victory conditions are not different for the 2 players (like in most Napoleonic games).

The game ends after 20 rounds and the object of the game is to score the most points, which are won by capturing opposing forces or occupying certain map points at the end of the game.

From the bottom of the box:

Kan Zen: the perfect word game

A GAME AND A WORLD:

KAN ZEN (tm) is more than a game. It is a world. In that world there is a place for everyone, and fun for everyone. There is also a place for all of the words on earth - those in your own language, those in foreign languages, ordinary words, and words that are the names of persons or places - if the letters of an alphabet can be used to spell them.

The game can be as easy, or as difficult, as the players choose to make it - among yourselves you agree on the kind of words that are to be used. The playing time can be readily adjusted to suit your convenience.

Above all, KAN ZEN is a world for those who let their imagination soar, discovering how much enjoyment they can have with a game that permits almost infinite variation.

KAN ZEN AT A GLANCE:

The board is a circular track, on which a step is taken for each letter of a word played. Letters drawn at random at the beginning of each move must be included in the words that you use. UP-TURNS, where the TALLY (letter count) is added to your score, alternate with DOWN-TURNS, where the TALLY is subtracted, unless evasive action is taken. The "perfect" words are those whose TALLIES enable you to move to sectors of the circle where the best advantage can be taken on multiple scoring opportunities on UP-TURNS or where losses can be avoided or minimized on DOWN-TURNS. The winner is the player with the highest net accumulative TALLY at the end of the game.

HOW MANY CAN PLAY?

Two, three or four sides can play the highly competitive versions of the The Perfect Word Game - as individuals, partners or teams.

The game can also be played "solo". Those who love words, or who find pleasure in drawing on a well-stocked memory of names of persons and places, may well consider KAN ZEN the best solitaire game in the world.
This is a card game from the 1950-60s. Players set a line-up and draw cards one at a time. Each card shows a result based on the player in the respective line-up slot. Normal baseball rules apply.

World Cup is a soccer game which is played in 2 phases, the arrangement of the teams phase and the soccer match phase.

There are 32 soccer player cards in 4 colors, as well as 12 Superstar cards and 12 Fan cards included. Each player is dealt 4 soccer player card, 1 of each color, and some money.

During Phase-1, the players walk around a 16 space course, spend money for soccer player cards, superstars and fan cards. They also need money to make transfers with other players or to replace soccer players, which have been hurt by injuries. The strength of all teams is always kept up-to-date, because the income depends directly on it!
As soon as all soccer playing cards are sold, Phase-1 ends and players with incomplete teams pay for making up their teams again. If they don't have enough money, they get the worst available cards.

Phase-2 is the match phase (money does not help here anymore): 
The matches are played on a 7x13 playing field. Each team has 3 tokens which merely serve as defenders. The ball is moved by putting cards on the table. The attacker must announce, which card(s) he wants to play and the defender can then arrange his tokens according to certain restrictions. Goals can only be realized by Superstars - but there are 12 in this game, so most matches will end with a winner. A match is finished, when one player has played all his cards.

All succeeding matches must be played with the same deck of cards, so it's clear, that Phase-1 is most important to become the final World Cup Winner.

Rymdimperiet (Space Empire in English) is one of the few games the Swedish publishers "Äventyrsspel" did on their own without licensing an already exciting American game. The gameplay in itself is simple and each player will play the part of a space trader shipping goods across the galaxy. The goal of the game is to generate a net of 150,000 credits and return to your home planet before any of the other players.

Players move on already existing trade routes which they later can choose to buy to make their own for a set price. Once a trade route has been bought it will earn that player revenue each time another player is forced to use that particular route, as well as create safe passage way for your own trading ship when traveling in the sense that you do not have to pay the expenses to another player and give them the edge.

Trade routes are different in length and usefulness and players will also be given the option to upgrade their ship to go in Hyperdrive, thus allowing them to move around the board to their destinations faster. The game uses a table where you look up the revenue from each trade between the starting planet and the goal for your shipment.

The element that makes the game difficult is that you cannot actively choose your trade route. It is randomly generated by dice rolls through a table to see to what area and which planet on the board you have to go to. This is also where owning and buying trade routes plays the part of a tactical element.

Re-implements:

    Rail Baron

First To Find! is a two to four person geocaching themed card game that can be played in 20-30 minutes. The game captures the excitement of the international sport, Geocaching!  The game features stunning, original artwork and comes in a collector's tin box!

Object:  Instead of standard suits, "FTF!" features cards suited to the seasons - fall, spring, summer, and winter. Players then use a combination of Gear (e.g. bug spray), Movement (e.g. sled) and Obstacle (e.g. spider) cards in the appropriate season of play to out-trick their opponents and race to become the first person to find the treasure, a.k.a "cache."
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
This is a space race game that uses a spinner for movement and has 3 types of cards for different spaces landed upon.  The goal is to complete a race around the solar system and back to earth.  Entering and leaving planets requires a special roll on the spinner.

It is patented - #3985361 and the text below is from the patent.

Abstract

A game device embodying a game board having a planar playing surface having a path of movement for game pieces delineated thereon, a control spinner and directional cards positioned on the game board for controlling movement of the game pieces. The path of movement is divided into a plurality of increments or areas some of which are provided with instructional indicia. The game pieces simulate rockets and the game board, spinner, control cards and indicia relate to and are simulative of space travel.

"Hi Score" was published in the German magazine Pöppel-Revue 5/86 as "Spiel im Heft".

The board has 6x6 spaces and both players have 18 stones, numbered from 1-18 on the front side and empty on the back side.

The game begins with an empty board. Each turn, a player places a stone on the board on the same row or column the last stone has been placed. Stones can not be moved to other spaces, but they are (must be) flipped over, if the sum of own stones in a row or column is higher than the sum of the opposing stones.

The game ends, when a player can not place a stone on the board. To determine the winner, the numbers on the not yet played stones are subtracted from the total on the stones on the board. The higher total wins.

Oförberedd ("unprepared") is a Swedish trivia game, but not quite like most trivia games.

The object of the game is to get through high school, get a doctorate, and finally win by becoming a professor.

When a player is still a student, his examiner (the next player) draws a card and reads a subject from that. The subject is something like "Name a city by the river ....". The examiner reads the question and fills in the blank with something of his own choosing. If the student can answer, he gets a diploma in that subject (there are four different subjects). If the student can't answer, the examiner must answer the question. If he can't, he gets a warning. Otherwise, the student gets the warning. The rest of the players are arbiters in the case of disputes as to what is the correct answer to a question.

After collecting three diplomas, the player is finished with high school and can now start working on his doctorate. To achieve that, he must get a diploma in each of the four subjects. Once he has that, he must also hold a lecture. To get the subject of his lecture, the player arranges his diplomas in a certain order and reads the sentence that this creates (if he has more than one diploma in a subject he may choose which one to use). This will result in a subject like "The bad influence / of movies / upon art / in troubled times." The student must now keep a two-minute lecture on this subject. If there are no obvious errors in his lecture, he gets his doctorate. To become a professor, the doctor must get another diploma in each subject and hold another lecture over a given subject.

Contents:
 - 48 student question cards
 - 16 doctor question cards
 - 12 warning cards
 - 64 diplomas
 - 6 "finished high school" counters (student's cap)
 - 6 "become a doctor" counters (doctor's hat)
 - 1 "become a professor" counter (laurel)
 - Rules booklet
 - Booklet with explanations of questions

The primary equipment for this game consists of four plastic trays which fit together in pairs, 252 plastic walls which fit between pillars in the trays, and two marbles.

Each player takes two trays and constructs a maze in one using the walls - some walls have large holes which the marbles can pass through, some have small holes which block them.  The second tray snaps onto the first to hold the walls in place.  The players swap mazes and compete to be the first to get a marble through theirs.

The game also comes with clamps which hold sets of trays together to make multi-level mazes - you can make a two-level maze with a single set.

The Battle of Stones River is a simulation of the battle of Murfreesboro in the American Civil War.

The game uses a fairly standard system reminiscent of SPI (Simulations Publications, Inc.) quads of the period.  The rules do have a few points of distinction.  For example, units have a stacking limit of two, and cannot attack on the same turn that a stack is created, so building up a concentrated assault takes time.  The advanced rules add different movement rules for different unit types and introduce battles which can continue over more than one turn.

The game is most notable for its map, which is full-color and mounted - highly unusual for a small wargame company at the time.  The map is 21.5"x22.5".  Besides the map, two rules booklets, and a die, the game contains 110 counters, of which 41 are blanks.

The board for SNAFU (which here is supposedly "Situation Normal All Filled Up" - sure) consists of 100 squares in 5 colors - red, blue, green, and yellow, all of which are assigned to the players, plus pink. Each player or team (the 4-player game is for teams) takes a color of chips, white or black.

On a turn a player spins a spinner which produces a result from 0-5 in one of the board colors or white.  If the color is pink or a friendly color, the player places markers in the number of spaces shown.  If an opposing color, the player may choose to place the opposing markers on the board.  If white, the player takes opposing markers off the board.

The game ends when one of four possible conditions are met - one team controls the main diagonal of the board, one team controls all squares of their colors, etc.  None of these conditions automatically win the game, but some add points to the team's score.

Scoop was one of Western's games distributed by Leisure Home Parties, which apparently was an early attempt at a home party sales approach to games. It has nothing to do with the Waddingtons/Parker Brothers game of the same name.

In this game, each player represents a newspaper trying to fill its front page, represented by a 6x8 grid. Players move around the main board buying, auctioning, and trading stories, which are thin tiles in four different sizes. The stories must be placed upright on the newspaper boards and must fit together without overlapping. Some stories have events on them, and all have fake headlines and leads.

boardgame: Slaughter & Carnage: Kursk's Stalingrad at Ponyri
description: two 11 x 17 mapsheets, 560 DTP counters. Tactical recreation of the battle of Ponyri, during July 7-10, 1943 at platoon level for tanks and company level for infantry and artillery. Lots of tanks on both side, on the German side, Ferdinand, Brummbar, Pz III, Pz IV, Marders and Sturmgeschutz, on the Russian side T-34, of course, plus KV-1, SU-152, T-60, T-70, and plenty of antitank guns and other artillery in addition to the infantry. Beautiful counters. Tense slugfest over a town located on the Northern salient of Kursk which is astride of the main German efforts
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Tonkin - La Guerre d'Indochine ("Tonkin - The Indochina War") covers the Indochine war from 1950, date of the take of power of Communists in China and the beginning of their support to Viet Minh troops, to 1954 and the battle of Dien Bien Phu.

It has four scenarios which can played individually or can be combined in a single campaigns, beginning with one of the four scenarios and ending the game in any other one.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
From the rules:

"As you travel the streets of New Orleans looking for pieces of the puzzle to solve the Candyman Murder mysteries you have to make sure you do not become a victim yourself. To win, player must proceed clockwise along the streets of New Orleans and get to the Mansion with the key card in order to unlock the secret to Candyman's power."

1 playing board, 1 die, 10 Hook Cards, 19 Candyman Cards, 1 Mansion Key Card, 30 Voodoo Cards, and 5 player tokens.

"Inspired by the imagination of the Gramercy Pictures Marketing Team and Designed by Jacobson & Atkins Advertising, Inc.  For Promotional & Publicity use only."

Game description by the publisher:
"A game by ASSASSIN, in co-production with Lutz Leischner. 
A nice boardgame with tin miniatures, which should not be taken too seriously. We just call it Monstern!"

The game includes several nice tin miniatures, a gameboard and several dice.

boardgame: Thurn und Taxis: Der Kurier der Fürstin
description:
The October (5/06) issue of Spielbox magazine included a small expansion for Thurn and Taxis.

Expands:

    Thurn and Taxis
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
In a colorful fantasy setting, players try to escape from the Schmunzelmonster (a tentacled alien monster). Each player has 4 Schlammhüpfers ('mud hoppers', the pawns), who try to escape from getting eaten by the Schmunzelmonster by throwing 2 dice. The first die determines if players get to move their Schlammhüpfers or the Schmunzelmonster (⅔ chance). The second die determines how far they must move. 
On a player's turn, if they still have a Schlammhüpfer in the safety of their burrow, they need to move it out before they may move another one on the board. Schlammhüpfer can cover quite a lot of distance because they skip occupied spots. That is needed, however, to get away from the ravenous monster.

The player with the last Schlammhüpfer remaining wins the game. Schmunzelmonster is a beautiful and colorful game for ages 6 and up.
family: Theme: Wetlands / Swamps / Bogs / Fens / Marshland

description:
Content:
27 picture cards, 4 value stones, 1 pawn, 1 dog, board

In this game a young boy walks around the strand and looks to see what he can discover there. When he meets his girlfriend he has to tell what he has seen.
The winner is the player who first reaches the field with the sun with his value stone.

Each turn players get tiles with pictures of common beach objects, and each turn they try to remember, in order, the previous tiles they have seen.  Remembering correctly earns you points, and the first to 11 points wins.

The game contains 160 cards and 85 notes and has got a mafia theme.  To win the game you have to be the richest player and to ruin the others.

Steal sports cars, repaint them, get into the sex industry, open a night club or a gambling club, it's all good to be full of dollars.
Make alliances, rob the bosses who are falling asleep on a large pile of bills, call the police and sell your enemies to justice. During the trial, take advantage of it by sticking a good piece of plastic in their casino, this will reset your competitions to zero!
The next day, when the poker game between bosses arrives, learn that with a few more aces, you always have a better game. The other players won't hesitate to cheat!
But be careful, don't insult a boss who plays honestly, he will make you pay dearly, very dearly!



Devenez le plus riche des caids an ruinant vos adversaires!
Volez des voitures de sport, repegnez-les, lancez vousdans l'industrie du sexe, ouvrez un night club ou un cercle de jeu, tout est bon pur etre full of dollars.
Faites des alliances, braquez les caids qui s'endorment sur un gros paquet de billets, appelez la police et vendez vos ennemis a la justice. Pendant le proces, profitez-en pur coller un bon pain de plastic dans leur casino, cela remettra lex competes a zero!
Le sour, quand arrive la partie de poker entre boss, apprenez qu'avec qeulques as de plus, on a toujous un meilleur jeu. Les autres jouers, eux n'hesiteront pas a tricher!
Mais attention, n'insultez pas un caid qui joue honnetement, il vous le fera payer cher, tres cher!

from Winsome Games:

"Wooden Shoes & Iron Monsters" is a Riding Series game by Han Heidema. WS&IM is set in the Netherlands and covers to history of the Dutch railroads from 1830 to 2006. WS&IM is for 3-5 players for about 2-3 hours. Players start railroads, capitalize them with shares of stock, build track from city to city, receive income and later merge smaller railroads into larger railroads. Prior games in this series include West Riding, Länderbahnen and Riding through England.

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Eastern US & Canada
description:
This Age of Steam expansion extends the base game Rust Belt map to include the Eastern USA and Canada, from Savannah, Georgia to Quebec, Canada. Both maps are used together to play this 3-6 player game. The game introduces the Marketing action and new rules concerning track building, interurban links, goods growth and production.
This expansion was released as a limited, numbered Essen Set by Winsome Games at Essen 2006. A thematic Turn Order marker was also included with the numbered Essen Set.

Expands:

    Age of Steam

boardgame: Kursk 1943: End of the Blitzkrieg, The Northern Pincer
description:
Main issue game contained in Alea Magazine issue 31.

Kursk 1943: The Northern Pincer, the End of Blitzkrieg (Part I) is a regiment-brigade simulation of the offensive of the 9th German Army during the battle of Kursk.

Colorful Caterpillars is a fun children's tactics game. The game is to get all of their own larvae first on to the game board. Players place their larvae on to the game board in turns. A great maneuvering game, where the larvae can be put this way, that someone else need to take theirs off or can't put their on to the board. Age range of more than 4 years.
Each player gets larvae pictured tiles, where in every single one of those is two holes, but they are in different places. One in head and tail of the larvae, or body and tail and so on. The tiles are in four different colors for each player. The game board have different colored circles on it, and your goal is to make your larvae complete by placing them in a way the circle match the color underneath (yellow to yellow). Win by getting rid of your tiles first.

The hilarious game that brings families together. This game is a fast paced-team game that provides hours of exciting game play while giving players opportunities to discuss family values, issues, tradition, and tasks in fun, laughter-filled environment. Teams race against the clock to come up with lists of answers to hundreds of statements and questions in 6 family oriented categories

Includes full color game board featuring the rooms and activities found in an ordinary house, 115 situation cards with close to 700 questions in 6 categories, 15 action cards with Bible verses, crazy actions and rewards, 2 playing pieces (plus 2 extra; 1 of each color), 30 second timer, die, and instructions

Mestarietsivä Basil Hiiri (Fin.) / Mästerdetektiven Basil Mus (Swe.) (English: "The Master-Detective Basil Mouse") is a board game based on the Walt Disney movie Basil the Great Mouse Detective. Players follow the footsteps of the great mouse detective through the streets of London as he chases Professor Ratigan and his gang.

In the game players use numbered cards to travel from one corner of the board to another via roads and rotating crossroads. Crossroads can be used to hinder other players. The goal of the game is to get into Big Ben (Clock Tower / now: Elizabeth Tower) when the clock hits twelve o'clock.

Stereo-5 is a 3-dimensional variation of 4-in-a-row game. Players use plastic pieces which can be attached to each other from six sides and they can be rotated if not "anchored" to main playing structure. Goal is to get 5 or more own colored pieces in a row in one of the three axis.

Trinity is a New Zealand designed and published abstract strategy game for 2 players.  The game is played on a 4 by 4 square grid, which is marked a-d on the horizontal axis and 1-4 on the vertical axis for record keeping.

There are three types of pieces, which are identical for black and white.  First are the ORBS, which are circular pieces with red, yellow, green or blue in the center. The second type of pieces are ARCS, which have a stripe of color matching those of the orbs.  The arcs are 120 degrees in extension, so three arcs fit around an orb.  HALOS are the third piece type, and are either white or black.  They are rings which fit outside the orbs and arcs.  There are four orbs, four arcs and six halos of each color, made of plastic.

The orbs are set up on the 1 and 4 rows with the matching color arcs with those orbs.  Play consists of each player moving one orb one square horizontally, vertically or diagonally.  The matching color of arc MUST be then moved.  Arcs can move to any other legal square on the board.

The easiest way to win is to trap an opposing orb with three arcs of any color around it (an Arc-win).  If you can box in your opponents orbs so that he has no valid moves, then you have gained an Orb-win.  Finally, if you can move your four pieces inside four halos of your own color you have completed a halo win.  A draw is an option through player agreement or stalemate from repeated moves.

A halo is "earned" by moving three of your arcs so that they form a complete ring, with no orbs inside it.  The arcs then attack the opposing pieces of the same strip color as the arcs.

The game rules come in a 16-page A6 sized rule book. There is also a CD for Windows PCs to practice strategies.

Which snail will win the exciting race, roll the right colors on their dice and reach the goal first? Watch out: Not only speed counts! If you run too fast you can easily stumble and get a knot on you snail's tail... and that costs time! Therefore remember, dear snail, that more haste makes less speed, and be careful so that you reach your goal safely.

Or, as they would say in Germany:

Welche Schnecke wird wohl das spannende Rennen gewinnen, die richtige Farbe würfeln und als erste ans Ziel gelangen? Doch aufgepasst: Nich nur die Geschwindigkeit zählt! Denn wer zu schnell läuft, kann leicht stolpern und einen Knoten in seinen Schneckenschwanz bekommen... und das kostet Zeit! Darum, liebe Schnecke, eile mit Weile und Bedacht, damit du heil am Ziel ankommst.

boardgame: Black Americans of Achievement: The Game
description:
This question and answer style game is played by groups of two to six. Players/partners "bet" on the success of the answers with points. The objective: answer your question correctly or guess whether another landing player will answer his or her question correctly, winning or losing points accordingly.

This game presents very positive role models in a way that heightens one's awareness of the achievements of Black Americans. Be inspired by the triumphs and struggles of both celebrated and unheralded Black Achievers who are part of this heritage of American success.

The box states that the game is "Based on Chelsea House Publisher's critically acclaimed 'Black Americans of Achievement' series of books, drawing from the expertise of the W.E.B. DuBois Institute of Afro-American Research, Harvard University", and was "sponsored by Burger King and Grolier Books".

This game is the graduation work of a student of visual communication in Weimar.
In the game players assume the roles of illegal immigrants trying to acquire a German passport while living from illegal work and evading the police.

Home Page: http://www.keinpasskeinspass.de/

translated from Dutch cover:
Choo Choo, The train is about to leave!

All aboard? The passengers have lost their luggage at different stations and the players have to help them find their luggage. This is achieved by traveling by train from station to station. Good memory may help you find your luggage. The player who is the first to find his or her three pieces of luggage wins.

It's a set of ninety cards in two categories: questions and answers.  From the box, it's a "merry question and answer card game for merry parties".

The questions begin with "What would you do if...".  The answers are along the lines of "I would soak it in milk".

Where's Wally? The Game is based on the Where's Wally books by Martin Handford who also designed this game.

The game is played by using a spinner and counters around a fixed board.

Aim: The aim of the game is to be the first player to reach the designated finishing place (in this case a large question mark) To achieve this, players must complete a series of observational questions outlined on the cards using the picture boards included.
This game simulates a soccer match. Two players roll dice, moves your teams and try to mark the biggest number of goals.

Nobelspelet is a Chutes and Ladders type of game with humorous (but still somewhat realistic) depictions of the steps in a scientific career. Players roll a golden six-sided die and move along the path from birth to the Nobel prize.  Good rolls move you ahead fast, poor rolls give you long detours or move you backwards.  Ironically (or not!) this game to win the Nobel prize requires no skill.  Published jointly by the Nobel Prize Museum in Stockholm, and Alga in Vittsjö, in honour of the 2001 Centennial of the Nobel prize.

The Casey Jones Game Box contains three games: Blow the Whistle, Capital Run, and Freight Train.  All games are played with a single die, and (at most) 20 train cars.  Blow the Whistle is a simple race game for two players, playable by children as young as three-years-old. Players roll and move, with the requirement that they make horn sounds at certain points in the game.  The first player to reach the finish line wins.  Capital Run is a game for older children, with up to four players.  This game also has a roll-and-move mechanic, and players must successfully name state capitals in order to earn a turn, with the object to cross the country on a preset path.  Freight train is light but nontrivial game for two players, requiring players to assemble a set of different types of trains.  These trains (coal tender, box car, flat car, cattle car) begin the game on preset spaces, and players must roll around the board and assemble their set.  There are several opportunities for decisions.

The object of the game is to travel from school through the neighborhood and arrive home safely with the most Safety Tokens. Along the way, players encounter real life situations that require safe and smart decision making. Opportunities to earn Safety Tokens by using code words, "Safe Spot" locations and buddying up present themselves with every turn, as well as opportunities to say "No" to the dangerous "Stranger". The more advanced version of the game utilizes Safety Situation cards, extra chances to earn Safety Tokens and emphasize the rules of personal safety.

Walt Disney's Davy Crockett Adventures, basically each player tries to collect all four Davy Crockett adventure cards onto his playing rack. 
These consist of: 'Davy Grins the Bear' , 'Davy Fights Redstick' ,'Davy Goes to Congress' , and 'Davy at the Alamo'
This collection process involves the spinning of a spinner (which is a picture of Fess Parker as Davy Crockett holding a rifle) when the rifle points to one of the adventure cards it is yours. The only catch is that you have to collect the adventures in the order that they happened.

Who Will Try to Slay the Dragon First? The object is to be the first player to slay the dragon. Players use a spinner to move. They are trying to collect enough "Dragon Cards" (20)so that they can enter the "Dragons Secret Cave". Once in the cave players do battle with the Dragon by spinning the spinner if you spin the number "one" the Dragon is has been slain, any other number and you have to make another round through the cave and try again.

This is based on one of the episodes in the movie The Wonderful World of the Brothers Grimm.

Object: Four different Popeye Adventures are illustrated on the board. The object of play is for the player to win each of the four adventures by "eating" enough cans of spinach (wooden green discs), which are won in the "Grand Spinach Lottery". After winning an adventure the player receives an Adventure Card. The first player to win all four Adventure Cards and get to the finish is the winner of the game. The game has a typical spinner, cards, wooden "cans of spinach", and a "Grand Spinach Lottery" which is a box that you shake tokens out of, the tokens state how many cans of spinach you will receive.

from the Box for the Tee Pee Toys version: All the Thrills of a Big Game: Running Plays, Passing Plays, Kick-Offs, Punts, Field Goals, Punt Returns.You are the Quarterback! Choose your own Plays! Follow the rules of football. Players each roll dice, the numbers on the dice refer to sequence tables which determine the results of the play (one player rolls for offense, and the other rolls for defense). Before rolling the offensive player must state whether he is going to run, kick, or pass. The game consists of "Four Quarters" four sets of (20) plays each quarter. The player with the highest score at the end wins.

Alternate version: Jr. Quarterback came with short game board, markers for downs and quarter of play, a cardboard football and a deck of 20 cards with plays. One quarter of play was one complete pass through the 20 plays card deck. Thus, the deck would be gone through twice per half and 4 times to complete one game. The offensive player would select a play. The card would be turned over to reveal the results. Following the rules of football i.e., 4 downs to gain 10 yards, 4 quarters of play, etc.) the ball would move up and down the field based on the play called and the associated gain or loss in yardage on the card associated with the play called. The plays that could be called were "left end", "left tackle", "left guard", "right end", "right tackle", "right guard", "short pass" and "long pass" as well as "punt", "kickoff" and "field goal". Each of the running plays had gains and losses as well as one fumble and a no gain. "Long pass" had four interceptions including one that was returned for a touchdown. "Long pass" also offered a touchdown. Short pass had a higher completion rate than long pass and only two interceptions. Jr. Quarterback is a fun game for two young players and can even be played by one. In my opinion ages elementary or for the adult who likes old school board games from a simpler time. Great game for teaching a young child the basics.
publishers: Tee Pee Toys, Jessup Paper Box Co.

description:
The Object is to be the first player to cross the street on the blue border "GIANT STEPS" and then back again to his colored "Start" space.
 Draw cards, do what they say in order to move. The game requires good manners to move, you must say "May I" of the person on the right, if you don't you lose your turn.

Object: Build up the "Careers" in the 8 pockets of the tray by playing matching Career Cards, numbered 1 to 6. Whoever spins the spinner to point at the matching Gold Key to Fame takes that career and scores for it later. The winner is the player with the highest score after 2 rounds. The game consists of a tray with eight circular slots, a center spinner on the tray which points to the slots and keys, circular "Careers" cards, and Eight gold keys representing each of the careers.

boardgame: Underground Railroad Perilous Journey to Freedom
description: Object: To become the first player to reach freedom in Canada, by answering trivia questions and avoiding the "Patroller" pawn who gets closer for each incorrect answer. Dice are used for only the first turn, after that movement is decided by the trivia cards. The papers inside the box state that there were only 1000 of this game produced.

These character/story based trading cards were made for spooky girls who have no interest in those angry little characters the boys seem to gravitate to. 16 orphaned girls each have their own unique story of how they became orphaned; each lost an object during their difficult time, developed a magic spell and adopted a pet monster. The girls are all somehow later involved in another tragic death. The cards have point values and there is a game that can be played regardless of how many cards have collected. 8 evil puppet cards are included in the full set.

More information:
http://www.therss.com/store/product_info.php?products_id=41&osCsid=f295fa51966c4cc1ccb021aaff82597f
http://www.therss.com/creepychristine/

A trivia game on antiques and collectibles.  Questions are given in three levels of difficulty: browser, buff, and advanced collector.  Each player chooses a level at the beginning of the game, thus allowing experts to play with beginners.  Each card has a relatively easy question (1 point if correct) and a relatively difficult question (2 points if correct).  Players announce their intention to answer the easy or difficult question before the next card is revealed.  First player to 30 points wins the game.

A sports trivia game that has been published in a collectible set of five decks of 50 cards each, each card with 4 questions, for a total of 1000 questions. Creator Juha Väätäinen is known as the European Champion of 5000m and 10000m running in 1971.

"The Child Making Game: Do you believe in the stork?"

From the back of the box:

The Child Making Game, intended for the 7th to 9th graders(* of the elementary school, is a fun teaching tool for the health and reproductive biology classes. The game has also been used a lot in youth work.

The game can lessen prejudices about pregnancy and add positive knowledge about pregnancy, childbirth and relationships. The effects of smoking, alcohol and unhealthy habits to the foetus and the expecting mother are informed about precisely and in a matter-of-fact manner.

The Child Making Game has been in use in schools for more than a decade, and has gained plenty of positive feedback from both teachers and pupils.

---


    ) 13-16 year olds.

Doctor Who collectible card game comprising 275 cards to collect, including a scratch 'n' sniff Slitheen card.  Released as a partwork each issue includes a pack with 9 cards (8 common and 1 rare).

Also available in booster pack form

The magazine is also a companion guide to the cards, featuring new gameplay rules, tips and tactics, monster fold-outs, episode guides and imagery from the Doctor Who TV series.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Dice assigns a color tile, tile color placed on board with matching color but more points awarded for longest pattern (line of the same colors--a "Coloraddo") created from reverse of tile.  When tiles are all on the board, the game is over and the winner is the one with the highest score.

Two types of categories, alphabet and general, make up the 99 different trivia categories.  Players try to reach one of the four finish spaces on the center circle and answers the general category question correctly wins.

Players answer questions about the Malcolm in the Middle television show while collecting money for right answers.  Wrong answers have the money going to a jackpot.  The first player to collect $100 wins.

Players begin an exciting journey as they take off from any of the six airports on the board.  During your tour, you'll be asked questions about the states you visit.  Answer correctly and you get a colorful souvenir card for that region you're visiting.  Be the first player to collect a souvenir card for all six regions and you're the winner.

Players must collect a complete set of 6 male or female sex organ cards.  To win with the male organs, he/she must collect 500 million sperm and then land on the motel. To win with the female organs, he/she must also have one unfertilized egg and land on the bedroom.

The male sex organs are:

Snake of Life
Jewel Plum of Naughty Desires
Valve of Relief
Horn of Plenty
Boulevard of Urgency
Sack of Containment

The female sex organs are:

Gates of Forbidden Pleasures
Avenue of Rapture
Digit of Delight
Tube of Sanctuary
Basket of Hidden Danger
Hall of Conception
ULTIMA is the lone non-card game included in Robert Abbott's excellent book "Abbott's New Card Games". It uses a chessboard & chess pieces, and is one of the more aberrant chess variants.

The game is a commercial game, related to Monopoly, for the purpose of promoting the area of the "Flensburger Förde", a landscape in the North of Germany.
The players move around a board, which consists of 40 squares. On most of the squares are parts of companies, which can be bought. The companies have values between 1000 and 8000 Euro. Three companies are combined by a color. Whenever a player lands on a win&loss-square, he must draw a card and the wins and losses of the business year are settled. According to the outcome the players gain money or have to pay money for their companies.
As soon as one player goes bankrupt, the game ends and all company cards are sold for their original price.

Patrol Torpedo

Patrol Torpedo is a game representing tactical naval combat between coastal forces during World War II.  The player assumes the role of motor torpedo or gun boat skippers and their opponents, up to and including destroyers and other small escort craft.

Game Scale and Conventions


The game is designed to be played using miniature figures and terrain.  The game is generally played using figures in the scale of 1/300 or 1/600 scale.1 turn - 50 Seconds
1 cm   - 25 Meters
1 Knot - 1 cm of movement

A game for families with children. The players move around the board, which consists of several pictures of trains and everything involved with them. Whenever a player hits a pictograph, he can draw one card of the 68 travel-cards. If it is a pictograph, it is placed according to its position on the board and the player can move again. Players can move with every pawn and in the end the player with the most travel-cards wins.

From the back of the box:

Rock to the top!  It's a race to stardom at the biggest rock concert of the year!  Take a spin to create a stylin' wardrobe!  Gather rockin' music and stock up on lots of bling!  The right combination will land you onstage, where you win the game- and you rock the spotlight!

A children's game, consisting of 32 cards with geese on both sides and a dice. The 32 cards are arranged in a spiral and all players start from the first card. After throwing the dice the player moves accordingly forward on the spiral. If he lands on a card, he has to turn it around and follow its instructions.
The card symbols are sometimes advantageous (e.g. take another turn), sometimes disadvantageous (e.g. sit out one turn). The players can also move other pawns of other players to move them on disadvantageous cards. Therefore it is essential to remember what text is on the backside of the cards. 
If two geese meet, one has to follow the orders from the backside of the card, the other must go back to the card where the first goose came from. The player wins, whose goose first passes the last card of the spiral.

Who will be the first to catch the fly in the middle?

Spiderrinth is a fun and tactical game, where the objective is to be the first to make a trail from one's home field inwards to the fly in the center of the web.

The trail is established by turning the treads and putting chips on the flies. When all the flies from one's home field to the center of the web is covered by a chip, you move your piece onto the prey in the center and thereby win the game - but only if you remember to say "Spiderrinth"!

(From the box - translated from Danish)

Dice determines how many spaces a player can move on the board.  When landing on certain locations a player may accumulate certain points.  Only 24 turns are allowed for each player as each round corresponds with an historical milestone leading to the eruption of Mt. Saint Helens on May 19, 1980 (edit: it was May 18th...).  It is possible for no player to win the game if all players are still in the red zone after the 24 historical milestones are reached.  Each historical milestone may give conditional instructions to players depending on where they are on the board.  Also, certain spots on the board may provide additional instructions...or instruct the player to draw from the emergency deck.

A player moves ahead on the board with a piece by rolling the die or pair of dice.  The landing space has a color.  A card of the same color is drawn.  If the player can correctly answer the question, the player can throw the dice and move ahead again.  Also, the player is given a puzzle piece matching the color/category if the player does not already have that piece.  The first player to complete a flag (using the puzzle pieces), return to California and answer a final question, wins.

Your rocket ship is a time machine made by Professor Smarty, the world's greatest scientist.  He wants you to journey back to the age of dinosaurs to catch the best ones for his new zoo - the DinoZoo.  If you do, he will make you Head DinoZookeeper.  You get points by catching dinosaurs and getting bonus chips.  The player with the most points at the end of the game is the winner.
An exciting game for all ages about Savannah!  Also includes $100 worth of coupons for local businesses!

Yet, another Monopoly (q.v.) clone with a marijuana theme. The differences between this and the older game are: players start with $1650 (instead of $1500), it only takes three of this game's version of houses to upgrade to this game's version of a hotel, there is a Police Car token that moves on everybody's turn in the opposite direction of the players. When the Police Car occupies the same space as a player then that player is sent to Rehab (this game's version of Jail).

Includes: Game Board, 5 Player Tokens, Play Money, 36 Kharma Cards, 27 Certificates of Stonership, 1 Police Car, 15 Plant Pieces, 32 Crop Pieces and 2 Stonerware Dice.
It appears that this game was produced without a Certificates of Stonership card for the Cheech's Bail Bonds space.

This is a children's game with a 3-D board (when the cardboard is properly assembled).  The most prominent part of the board is the large slide along one side of the board.

From the box: 
"Side Splitting Hi-Jinks with The 3 STOOGES in a Real Zany Fun House!"

Domorino is a color recognition and strategy game. Children create their own set of Dominoes using colored chips and domino frames and think more strategically. The first person who places all of his colored chips wins.

Age: 4+

Pisa is a strategy and fine motor skills game. Roll the dice to see how many pieces must be used. Start by building one level and move up to building four. Try to avoid a leaning tower as you carefully place bamboo pieces to support more levels.

Age: 4+

From the cover:
"A Pop-O-Matic game for younger children" (ages 4-9)

Dunce hats of four different colors square off by lining up along each edge of the square board with a Pop-O-Matic bubble in the middle.

Maselino is a game of speed. 40 Colorful bamboo discs are distributed by color to players who pretend they are mice looking for food. The players turn the tiles over until their pictures match. When they do, the players rob the piece of food from the plates in the middle. Only the quickest one can keep it. The winner is the one who gets the most pieces of food.

Age: 4+

Scoot is a children's spin-and-move game.  Featuring smiling children on the cover, the gameboard is a yard with a winding sidewalk up to the front door of a house.  The house stands up for a 3-d game board.

Scoot on home now!

From the front cover:
"New! Fast! Different! Exciting! Fun!"

Battelo is a memory and strategy game. Players move their panda bear through the forest according to the memo tiles they draw and try to be the first to collect 4 different colored leaves in their backpacks.

Age: 4+

boardgame: Tom and Jerry: Adventures in Blunderland
description: This is a children's roll and move game (actually, spin and move) based on the cartoon Tom and Jerry.  Four colored spinners are included with the game.  The pieces are cardboard stand-ups for moving around the board.

boardgame: Godfathers & Goodfellas: The Organized Crime Trivia Game
description: "Godfathers & Goodfellas" is a trivia board game that is driven by the popularity of the "wiseguy" genre, particularly in movies and on TV series such as “The Sopranos.” The game consists of a series of categories relevant to the 'mob scene' with questions in each category. The player is a good guy -- a special agent assigned to break up the mob by answering questions. With every correct answer, down goes another bad guy. The harder the question the bigger the bad guy … until the whole gang is gone.

"COWBOY RIDERS AND INDIANS" is as fresh and exciting as a ride across the Western plains. A brightly dec. orated metal wheel spins off numbers. Four "Wild West" riders move across hostile Indian country, in an effort to reach the safety of the military fort. The printed story along the trail is exciting and results are different each time it is played.

— from an advertisement

Avesta! is an abstract game themed loosely on the Zend Avesta - the sacred texts of Zoroaster (also called Zarathustra). It is chess-like in the powers and types of moves available, but there are a few spaces on the board that are limited to certain pieces, some pieces are used for blocking only, and some pieces are promoted when others are captured, which can tip the balance, making a capture or sacrifice critical to the outcome. There are two winning conditions: either the capture of the opponent’s offensive pieces, or the defilement (occupation) of your opponent’s temple for a full turn.

Remember the clowns clothes!

Objective for the game:
Try to remember how the clown on the clown card was dressed, and give the clown on the basic card the same clothes before the timer runs out!
The player who has collected the most points after 10 turns wins.

(From the box - translated from Danish)

From the advertisement:

Gift of Enlightenment is a unique and totally original board game that brings a flow of positive energy to all who discover it. Friends and family will be delighted by its healing messages and fun game play. This is one adventure that is guaranteed to stay in the mind long after the final dice has been thrown.

Game Features: - Seek your very own ‘Gift of Enlightenment’. Make life-changing decisions as you travel the ‘Circle of Life’.  Win and lose positive and negative human traits along the way.  Harness the energy around you. It can help you to reach the ultimate goal.  How powerful are your Sixth Sense, Insight, and Intuition?  Here’s your chance to find out as you seek to master the ‘Seven Chakra Challenges’.  Collect the Seven Chakra Crystals that can help you to open the wonderful ‘Book of Enlightenment’.

Delve into the mysterious Karma Pool.  Try to avoid falling for False Idols.  Gain a personal gift from the ‘Book of Enlightenment’.

Each beautifully boxed game contains: -

Game board

7 special Chakra stones with velvet holding bag.

6 Aura cards

3 packs of game cards

42 Chakra crystals (acrylic)

7 Chakra color cards

7 Chakra Challenges.

Wooden Dice

Full Instructions

Plus the 82 page ‘Book of Enlightenment’

boardgame: Little Angels and the Fruit of the Spirit
description:
Little Angels and the Fruit of the Spirit

As players move their little angels along the brightly colored path, they will be introduced to the names and meanings of all nine Fruits of the Spirit from Galatians 5:22.

Steward

In STEWARD, your mission is to manage and multiply the riches that have been entrusted to you.

Great riches are in your hands • What will you do with them

Make different purchase in order to produce olive oil, clay pots, food grains, clothes and more. Buy and export stone to build houses or temples or become the biggest copper producer in your area.  Discover the Biblical Principles that will make you a Great Steward! Never forget to tithe on all your income. It will be the key to your prosperity. Use strategy to succeed in the challenge that has been entrusted to you and by doing so become THE BEST STEWARD.

STEWARD • A captivating game that you will certainly enjoy and that will be the source of many unforgettable moments with your family and your friends.

boardgame: Professor Noggin's The Human Body Card Game
description:
Do you wonder about sneezing, saliva or your sense of smell? Ever ponder scabs, snoring or sweat? Then this game, all about YOU, is for you!

Professor Noggin's series of educational games encourages kids to learn interesting facts about their favorite subjects.  Each of the thirty game cards combines trivia, true or false, and multiple choice questions.  A special three-numbered die is included which creates interaction and promotes communication between players.  Easy and hard levels keep kids interested and challenged - while of course having fun!

Parts of Speech Challenge reinforces knowledge of nouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, prepositions, pronouns, conjunctions and interjections! 
 Parts of Speech Challenge helps players master the eight basic parts of speech plus verb phrases, prepositional phrases, articles, and proper nouns. Players learn how the same word can represent different parts of speech based on how it is used in a sentence.
 As they move around the board, drawing cards and reading sentences, players identify the parts of speech of selected words. All words are used in sentence context for meaningful learning.
 The Reference Sheet, included, allows players to familiarize themselves with the parts of speech. Younger players can use the Reference Sheet while playing the game.
 The object is to cover the most spaces on the board before one player runs out of markers. The player with the highest score wins!

description:
A simple and fun dice game for young children!  Players roll five dice with animal pictures (lion, monkey, giraffe, crocodile, elephant and hippo) on each side, and try to collect sets of matching animals.  The "Lion" picture is "wild".  After the first roll, players are allowed to re-roll the dice of their choice up to two times.  Players then place the equivalent number of matching animal tokens on their playing board.  This process is repeated for each player five times.  The winner is the player with the most monkey, giraffe, crocodile, elephant and hippo tokens on their playing board!  Suitable for children age 4 and older.

Contents:

     4 play boards 
     100 chips 
     5 dice 
     1 dice cup 
     1 sticker sheet 
     instructions

Hickery Dickery Clock is an easy game for children age 4 and up, about telling time and matching funny faces.

Object: Race against Timothy Mouse by striking each hour of the clock before turning up all 4 Timothy Mouse cards.  Players win by matching all the hours on the clock before the last Timothy Mouse card is turned up.

Contents: 

     5 piece jigsaw game board with clock hands
     deck of game cards with clock pictures

In Mithril, each player plays a team of dwarves exploring a mine to discover buried valuables, such as gold, beer, mithril and a treasure chest. Players must dig carefully to avoid being crushed in a cave-in, as the mines' stability is unpredictable, and the other players can sometimes trigger cave-ins and other hazards.

The game ends when a players brings the treasure chest back to his entrance. Then, the richest player wins the game.

The game is still under development.

Flink's unique gameplay combines the fun of shuffleboard with the skill of pool! Using the "Flink sticks", players "flink" discs to be nearest the backboard. Knock your opponent off the board, into the "gutter" and out of play! The player whose disc is closest to the backboard after flinking five discs each wins that round. The player who wins five rounds first has won the game.

Players must think of solutions to the stressful situations that they meet in the game and then discuss the results.  Everyone wins in Stress Strategies by listening, sharing, and accepting the feelings and responses of all the players, and by learning healthy ways to manage stress.

Based on the popular book by Michael Ende. One player takes the role of Jim Knopf, who wants to take as many friends as possible with him in Lukas' - who is played by another player - locomotive Emma. But even in Lummerland - the imaginary island, where the train tracks lead around - you need a ticket to ride the train. And it isn't as easy to get one of those...

This is a very simple roll-and-move game from the former East Germany (DDR) -- the game is pictured in the Taschen Icons Book _DDR_Design_.  From the appearance in the book, it's a bedtime game for children -- players roll the die and move their piece, and follow the directions when they land on certain spaces -- e.g. move forward, wait for two turns, etc.

John Blund is known in Scandinavian as a mythological little man who calms the children and helps them to sleep.

The goal of the game is to reach the finish point first. Just roll the die and move. If you end up on a "action"-field just follow the simple instructions.

Zzzymble is a card game inspired by Sudoku and is a fast paced card game that is fun for all ages.

The box contains cards with five suits, containing the numbers 1-9, plus various special cards that affect gameplay. Players are trying to play their cards into a 5x5 grid. Cards may not be played adjacently (orthogonally or diagonally) to another card that match either number or color. When the grid is full, players sum card values remaining in their hands. Over a number of rounds (playing to 100 points), the winner is the player with the fewest points.

A planned forthcoming expansion is called "It's as Zzzymble as Zzzat."

The board shows the German Alps and the villages and cities situated in that area. The different locations are connected by lines.
The players draw a card and place a flag into the according location. In addition they draw cards from every color on the board, which then represents their travel route. Other cards are telegram cards (describing a special occurrence) and pass cards (which are used to travel over mountains).
The players roll a dice and move accordingly. Whoever manages to visit all his locations and returns to his starting point first, wins.

Help Pip & Pop, Tutter and Ojo find their favorite things hidden in Bear's yard.  Spin to move ahead.  Land on special spaces and turn over Bush tokens to see what's hidden where.  Find two of your character's favorite things and reach Bear's house first to win.

Anti-Grid is an abstract strategy game for two players, each trying to eliminate the other’s pieces through capture. The unique thing about the board is that each player plays on his/her own grid - they never follow the same paths to victory or defeat. Capture is made by flanking, or trapping an opponent’s piece between two of your own, and players may set up “barricades” to impede their opponent’s moves (momentarily, anyway). There are two boards included with a larger and a smaller grid, allowing shorter or longer, more challenging games.

Each player spins to establish their color.  The 24 puzzle pieces are evenly distributed.  The red player starts by trying to put the puzzle together while the person on their left is trying to spin red's color.  When red is spun, the player spinning tries to put the puzzle together while the person on their left tries to spin that persons color.  The first player to use all of their puzzle pieces is the winner.

Players are dealt all of the cards in the deck and the starting player takes a card from the person on their left.  They can either kiss that body part of the person who they took the card from or leave it in their hand.  When you lose all of your cards, you are safe.  When the next to last player is out of cards the loser is the one holding the Kiss My Ass card.
Crazy 8ths is a music based educational card game that introduces many variants to popular card games, and is designed to help children learn to read music.

Classic Mad Gab goes digital as Mad Gab Mania adds visual and audio clues as well as other challenges that creates a whole new way to play.  Programmable Game Buzzers let teams compete head to head for lightning-quick play!

Features:
- Steal points by unscrambling "Scramble Swipes."
- You make the choice and control the game with "Pass or Play."
- If you're behind, you can get help from "The Equalizer."

Another Monopoly variant.  Utilities are replaced by observatories, railroads by telescope manufacturers and properties by celestial bodies.  The pewter pieces are: radio telescope, telescope in dome, Hubble Space Telescope, Schmidt-Cassegrain telescope, binoculars and lawn chair.

“Cribbage - The Board Game” is a simple, fast game for anyone who enjoys playing the traditional cribbage card game. Unlike the traditional card game, the board game can only be played with two teams and therefore cannot be played by three players. Fortunately (and uniquely) this cribbage game can be played very well with six players forming two teams of three. Each team alternates play by placing a card on the board from their dealt hand. The board is a five by five grid with a face down card in the center revealed only after all cards are played. At the end of each round one team will score the cribbage “hands” that form the “rows” while the other team scores the cribbage “hands” that form the “columns”.  Every card placed will become a card in one of your cribbage “hands” on the board but will also be a card in one of your opponents “hands”. The trick therefore is to strategically place cards in such a way to help yourself but hinder your opponent. The board also has card values printed on some spots for instant scoring. Placing a queen on a spot on the board with a queen printed on it will score an instant two points - just as matching a pair during regular play would in a traditional cribbage game. Placing a five on the same queen spot would similarly score an instant two points for the fifteen. Final scoring is done after all spaces are filled using the same procedure one uses for a traditional cribbage hand. First team to 121 wins. If you like to play cribbage, especially with a group, this is a fun twist on one of the world’s oldest card games.

Quoted from the game box: “BOK is a fast paced strategy game that is more demanding and challenging than Backgammon or Chess. Truly a remarkable and intriguing concept, unparalleled in the history of games.  BOK will remain unchallenged in years to come as The Ultimate Strategy Game.”

This game is one of the winners of the 2006 Boulogne-Billancourt International Designer Contest.

In Panama, players are mission leaders that strive to complete the first canal joining the Atlantic and Pacific oceans. Three projects are under study: one in Panama itself, another one in Mexico and one in Nicaragua.

To win the game, the players must not only dig the canals, but they must also hire experts, emit lotteries to fund their mission, buy shares in the different projects, and invest wisely in the six resources required when building a canal.

Salut Les Filles! must be played within a public area, such as a pub or coffee.

Players play cards describing a physical element that can be found on a person, such as long hair, cell phone, she kisses me, etc...

The first player to spot a person with at least one attribute on a card gets the card and marks a point, other players have to agree if the person corresponds with the card's element.

A trivia type game where players must move inward along the different color tracks until one player reaches the mortarboard space while trying to figure out definitions to words.  The first player who picks the correct definition of the word while on the mortarboard space wins the game.

Each player takes a net and on the count of three, presses four grasshoppers down on the board as fast as they can. In a few seconds, the suction cups will loosen and the grasshoppers will hop high into the air. The player who catches the most in his or her net wins.

Start by preparing the cheatwell punch (recipe included).  Each person turns over one of their cards in front of them trying to find a match on the board between players.  When a match is spotted, Snap is called.  The two players who match, race to be the first to call their opponent something in the opponent's category beginning with 'A'.  The winner of the Snap does not have to drink.

Players compete in a test of knowledge, trying to win the most money by answering questions in the category of their choice.

Based on the TV game show of the same name, hosted by Mike Darrow (pictured on box) for its first season (1976-77).

Take over the house with the help of creepy crawlers bugs.  You can play with bugs inside this box, or with bugs you make in your creepy crawlers oven.  Players take turns moving, capturing room after room as their bugs swarm through the house.  The first player to capture three rooms wins the game.

From the back of the box:

Welcome to the Happy Valley 314th ward! Does it look familiar? We have six nursery classes, two independently running Primaries, and two elders quorums. We’re packed like sardines into our ward building and it’s time to Split the Ward! In this fun, action-packed game, you and your friends and family have been called to help rearrange a cast of zany characters into new leadership positions as quickly as possible. The first person to organize three completed presidencies wins! The action cards and wild cards will keep things interesting and you’ll be surprised at how many of the “peculiar people” in our ward family remind you of someone in your own ward! Find out how much fun it is to Split the Ward!
family: Religious: Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints

description: Players form words using cards.  They meld them for points starting with a 5 letter word.  Three letter words can be added to to form a new word.  The first player to reach 5,000 points wins.

boardgame: The Original Game of Rudolph the Red-Nosed Reindeer
description: Take Rudolph's place at the head of Santa's sleigh as you guide the team through adventure-filled skies.  Learn to be a leader and to share.  Pick up action cards along the way with messages that give children of all ages life-long lessons about teamwork, diversity, consideration and more.  Look for Rudolph's red nose to appear magically on select action cards.  Be the first to place all your Rudolph gifts under the tree and win.

Players start on the letter A space and roll a colored die to determine if they pick a card.  If they don't pick a card, they have to name an animal starting with the letter A.  They advance to B and repeat the process until either they roll the color yellow, can't name an animal or land on an alpha card box.  An advanced version gives each of the six colors on the die a different question that the players must answer.

Mindrubble is  a combination of abstract gaming and trivia games. That is: to make a move on the board, you have to answer a trivia question first. The game has 400 question cards in 3 categories: numbers, cities, people. Furthermore there is a board with a 8x8 grid, containing symbols of the 3 categories and 64 chips, grey on one side, black on the other.

Before starting the game 2 gray and 2 black chips are placed in the middle of the board. If you have 4 players, you'll play in 2 teams. On your turn you place a chip in your color on the board next to one of your opponent. Your goal is to capture your opponents chips.

How is that done? First you try to close your opponents chips in. For that you need to have your chips on both sides of your opponents. This can be horizontal, vertical or diagonal. Then you try and capture the chip(s) by answering a question. When answered correct, the opponents chip(s) are turned over. When answered incorrect, nothing happens and your opponent takes his/her turn.

The game ends when the board is full or when no player can place a chip or when all chips are one color. The player with the most chips up, wins.

boardgame: Bulldogs Away!! Modern Fast Attack Craft Rules 1950 - 2010
description:
Modern Fast Attack Craft Rules

by David Manley

Current Edition 1.0

36 pages; recommended scale 1/600, 1/700; complexity: 6/10; level of detail: 5/10; avg game length 2 hours; dice required: D20, D10, D6

View Summary of Contents

The rules are for the recreation of small naval engagements with Fast Attack Craft such as missile boats and smaller warships, mainly in coastal waters. The period covered runs from the late 1950’s onwards, and allows also for the use of aircraft and helicopters.

An important aspect of the rules is that the units can start as unidentified “blips”, which might be dummies, and there is a simple detection and identification system that changes the status to “contact” (detected as an enemy vessel) and “target” where the model is deployed. If you can get out of radar “sight” then you can revert to “blip” status again.

The shooting system allows for guns and missiles of various types, and damage is recorded in a graded system with 5 states from intact to sunk. Vessels can take Special hits when damaged which further degrade their ability. There are simple rules for operating helicopters from ships, as well as rules for anti aircraft fire with guns and missiles.

There are also additional rules for unconventional craft, mine warfare and operations against coastal targets.

They are rounded off with extensive data tables for missiles, torpedoes, guns, rockets, SAM systems, Air to Air missiles, ships, helicopters and aircraft. (There are 140 sets of ship data from over 40 countries).

Summary of Contents

INTRODUCTION

SETTING THE SCENE: Vessel Descriptions; Command and Control; Play Area

TURN SEQUENCE AND INITIATIVE

MOVEMENT: Moving Astern; Turning; Collisions; Drifting; Grounding; Towing

TARGET DETECTION AND IDENTIFICATION: Detection / Identification States; Managing “Blips” and other markers;  Moving “Blips” and “Contacts”; Notes on Detection and Identification; Radar Detection; Detecting Radar Emissions; Sharing Detection Data; Visual Identification of the Target; Random Visibility; Sharing Identification Data; Starshells and Flares; Countermeasures

FIRING: Weapon Firing Arcs; Eligibility of Targets (Lines of Fire); Gunfire; Missile Fire; Torpedo Fire; Salvo Rocket Launchers and Rocket Pods

DAMAGE:  Basic Damage Table; Damage Control

AIRCRAFT AND HELICOPTERS:  Altitude; Aircraft and Helicopter Detection and Identification; Surface to Air and Air to Air Fire; Aircraft Damage; Air to Surface Attacks; Launching and Recovering Helicopters

UNCONVENTIONAL CRAFT: Semi Submersibles; Surface Effect Ships; Hydrofoils; Hovercraft; Ekranoplans; Suicide Craft

MINE WARFARE: Types of Mines; Hits on Minelayers; Mine Laying; Detonation; Minesweeping; Mine Hunting; Minesweeping Helicopters

LAND FORCES: Troops; Vehicles; Buildings; Use of Naval Weapons against land targets

TABLE OF ACRONYMS AND ABBREVIATIONS

DATA TABLES: Air to Air Missiles; Anti-Ship Missiles; Guns and Rocket launchers; SAM Systems; Special Craft; Torpedoes (Unguided); Torpedoes (Guided); Bomb Systems; Typical Helicopters; Typical Aircraft; Notes on Ship Data Tables

SHIP DATA TABLES

The game consists of a deck of thirty-two oversized playing cards. Each card depicts a sheriff, a gunman or an ordinary cowboy. Each player is dealt a card and looks at it secretly.

When it is a player's turn, that player returns the card to the bottom of the pile and draws another if it is not a gunman or a sheriff. If a player has a gunman, that player can choose to eliminate another player by saying, "Stick 'em Up!" If the chosen player responds by revealing a card whose cowboy doesn't have his hands up that player is eliminated. Responding with a gunman or sheriff eliminates the player who is taking a turn.

If a player reveals a sheriff on his turn, all players holding gunmen are out of the game. The last player remaining is the winner.

description:
Naval Wargames Rules for Coastal Forces
1914 – 1953

by David Manley

Current Edition 3.2

40 pages; recommended scale 1/600; complexity: 7/10; level of detail: 6/10; avg game length 2 hours; dice required: D10, D6
